inventory_name: Well Control, Well Barrier and BOCP* 25-01-01-00-V5-B9
comment_self: Level 1:Well Control is the process of managing wellbore pressure to prevent the uncontrolled flow of formation fluids (such as oil, gas, or water) into environment and avoid blowouts during drilling completion, production, or workover operations. Primary barrier - Control formation pressure by adjusting drilling fluid density to maintain greater hydrostatic pressure in the wellbore. i.e. Kill fluid (Muds – SBM) Secondary Barrier- Install and activate BOPs at the wellhead to isolate the wellbore and contain pressure when a kick occurs. i.e. Annular BOP, Ram BOPs (Pipe Rams, Blind Rams, Sher Rams), Hydraulic BOPs and Manual BOPs. Ref: PTS 25.01.03 Well Barriers and Well Control.Allow formation pressure to push fluids into the wellbore when hydrostatic head is insufficient, triggering a kick and raising surface pressure. Safely manage the kick by evaluating kick tolerance—based on well design, fluid properties, and pressure limits. Continuously monitor and adjust mud weight to maintain adequate hydrostatic pressure and prevent kicks.
comment_supervisor: He demonstrated a strong theoretical understanding of well control, gained through self-study of governance and project documents. He was able to explain key concepts such as well control barriers, pressure relationships, kick indicators, and high-risk operations.Although he has no direct field experience with well control issues at his rig, he was able to describe well control methods and the required competencies for personnel involved in site response. To further strengthen his knowledge, continued self-reading particularly on Well Control Response Team (WCRT) requirements is recommended. Agreed to close this ruler for “awareness” at this stage. Recommended for him to participate in a WCRT drill to gain practical experience in well control response.

comment_self: Level 1:Conducted multiple HSE Pre-Mobilization briefings prior to the commencement of each project, with particular emphasis on supporting Exploration HSE activities in 2023. During these sessions, the crew onboard was briefed on the PETRONAS Substance Misuse Policy. The key points discussed included:a) The company's commitment to maintaining a workplace free from drug and alcohol abuse.b) The enforcement mechanisms of the policy.c) The compliance expectations for both PETRONAS personnel and contractors. Conducted Pre-Mobilization Briefings as a platform to communicate the impact of substance misuse in the workplace and emphasize the importance of compliance with relevant policies. The session also covered the consequences of non-compliance under the company's consequence management framework.a) Substance misuse can impair normal brain functions, leading to changes in perception, memory, coordination, concentration, mood, consciousness, cognition, and behavior. These substances pose serious risks to both short- and long-term health due to their mind-altering effects.b) Individuals under the influence may lose control over their thoughts and emotions, making them susceptible to irrational behavior. This increases the risk of health and safety incidents, potentially endangering both themselves and others in the workplace. Conducted verification of Substance Misuse testing as part of the project's Pre-Mobilization process, specifically focusing on drug and alcohol screening. The testing was carried out onboard by the Assistant Medical Review Officer (AMRO) prior to the vessel's departure to the site. Test results were reviewed and vetted before granting clearance for the vessel to proceed.This requirement is outlined in the HSE Exhibit – Standard HSE Requirement
comment_supervisor: Steeve meets the Awareness Level (Level 1) requirement for the Substance Misuse Ruler. He demonstrates the ability to describe in basic terms the relevant PETRONAS policy, the health and workplace impact of substance misuse, and the testing procedures applicable to site operations.
